sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain in his right index finger, rated 7 out of 10, following a skiing injury. He describes the injury as occurring while double-black diamond skiing, where he lost control and flipped down a few ways. He reports no other injuries. The patient reports chronic constipation and takes miralax. He denies any chest pain, dyspnea, or syncope.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation of the right index finger joint. Range of motion is limited. X-rays demonstrate no fractures or dislocations in the right hand.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a sprain of the distal interphalangeal joint (DIP) of the right index finger. The primary problem is a sprain of the right index finger.
medical_concepts: sprain of the distal interphalangeal joint of the right index finger
sprain of the right index finger

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The doctor will apply a splint to the right index finger. The doctor will prescribe 14 milligrams of mobic once daily. The patient will return for follow-up in two weeks. Order the medications and procedures mentioned.

medical_concepts: sprain of the distal interphalangeal joint of the right index finger
sprain of the right index finger
